title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. pratensis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. pratensis, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of menstrual disorders, and its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties have been utilized to soothe wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. pratensis include its ability to reduce menstrual cramp pain, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and support the healing of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. pratensis is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make infusions, ointments, and salves for topical application.
